Title: Thylakoid localized bestrophin-like proteins are essential for the CO2 concentrating mechanism of Chlamydomonas reinhardtii
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports CO2 concentration around Rubisco, improving photosynthetic carbon fixation and reducing photorespiration under low CO2
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bestrophin-like membrane protein with 4 predicted transmembrane domains and chloroplast transit peptide
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast thylakoid membrane, enriched near pyrenoid periphery and pyrenoid tubules
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated within 2 h after shift from high CO2 to ambient/low CO2; expression remains elevated for at least 12 h; lower expression than BST2/BST3; induction depends on CIA5
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated under ambient/low CO2; reduced in cia5 mutant
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi triple knockdown of BST1-3; RT-qPCR; semiquantitative RT-PCR; Venus localization; growth assays under low CO2; Ci affinity via oxygen evolution; 14Ci uptake/accumulation assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes redundantly to CCM function; reduction together with BST2 and BST3 lowers Ci affinity, Ci uptake/accumulation, and growth under low CO2
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PSAD constitutive promoter for Venus fusion localization constructs
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High CO2 (5%); ambient CO2 (0.04%); low CO2 (<0.02%); pH 7.8 and 8.4; light
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduction caused small decrease in proton motive force but no major photodamage and normal Fv/Fm under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime candidate thylakoid bicarbonate transporter for engineering algal CCM into plants to improve photosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BST1 is one of three low-CO2-induced thylakoid bestrophin-like proteins in Chlamydomonas. 2. It localizes to pyrenoid-associated thylakoid membranes. 3. Simultaneous knockdown with BST2 and BST3 impairs growth at low CO2, reduces Ci affinity, and reduces Ci uptake, supporting a role in stromal-to-lumen bicarbonate transport for the CCM.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-gene evidence for BST1 was not isolated; function inferred from highly similar triple-knockdown analysis and redundancy among BST1-3.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics referenced from prior study (Fang et al. 2012); interactome referenced from prior study (Mackinder et al. 2017); accession numbers NA

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports CO2 concentration around Rubisco, improving photosynthetic carbon fixation and reducing photorespiration under low CO2
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bestrophin-like membrane protein with 4 predicted transmembrane domains and chloroplast transit peptide
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast thylakoid membrane, enriched near pyrenoid periphery and pyrenoid tubules
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated within 2 h after shift from high CO2 to ambient/low CO2 and remains elevated for at least 12 h; induction reduced in cia5
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated under ambient/low CO2; reduced induction in cia5 mutant
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi triple knockdown of BST1-3; RT-qPCR; semiquantitative RT-PCR; Venus localization; growth assays under low CO2; Ci affinity via oxygen evolution; 14Ci uptake/accumulation assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes redundantly to CCM function; reduction together with BST1 and BST3 lowers Ci affinity, Ci uptake/accumulation, and growth under low CO2
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PSAD constitutive promoter for Venus fusion localization constructs
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High CO2 (5%); ambient CO2 (0.04%); low CO2 (<0.02%); pH 7.8 and 8.4; light
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduction caused small decrease in proton motive force but no major photodamage under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful CCM component candidate for engineering bicarbonate delivery into thylakoids in plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BST2 is a low-CO2-responsive thylakoid bestrophin-like protein. 2. It colocalizes with pyrenoid-associated thylakoid membranes. 3. Joint knockdown with BST1 and BST3 compromises low-CO2 growth and Ci uptake, consistent with bicarbonate transport in the CCM.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Formerly reported as LCI11 together with BST3 in prior literature; individual knockout phenotype not tested here.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics referenced from prior study (Fang et al. 2012); interactome referenced from prior study (Mackinder et al. 2017); accession numbers NA

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports CO2 concentration around Rubisco, improving photosynthetic carbon fixation and reducing photorespiration under low CO2
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bestrophin-like membrane protein with 4 predicted transmembrane domains and chloroplast transit peptide
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast thylakoid membrane, enriched at pyrenoid periphery; native promoter construct showed same pattern
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced within 2 h after transfer to ambient/low CO2 and elevated for at least 12 h; expression severely reduced in cia5
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated under ambient/low CO2; reduced in cia5 mutant
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: bst3 single knockout; RNAi triple knockdown of BST1-3; RT-qPCR; immunoblot for BST3 protein; semiquantitative RT-PCR; Venus localization under PSAD and native promoter; growth assays; Ci affinity assays; 14Ci uptake/accumulation assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single bst3 knockout shows near-normal low-CO2 phenotype, but reduction together with BST1 and BST2 strongly impairs CCM, indicating functional redundancy and essential collective role in Ci uptake/accumulation
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PSAD constitutive promoter for Venus fusion; native BST3 promoter for localization validation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High CO2 (5%); ambient CO2 (0.04%); low CO2 (<0.02%); pH 7.8 and 8.4; bicarbonate-depleted conditions; light
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single knockout has weak phenotype; knockdown causes small pmf reduction but no severe photodamage
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime candidate CCM thylakoid bicarbonate transporter for transferring CCM modules to crop chloroplasts
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BST3 localizes to thylakoid membranes and is enriched near the pyrenoid, including under its native promoter. 2. A bst3 single knockout has only weak phenotype, indicating redundancy. 3. Combined reduction with BST1 and BST2 severely reduces low-CO2 growth, Ci affinity, and Ci accumulation, supporting an essential collective transporter role in the CCM.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CLiP mutant identifier LMJ.RY0402.089365 used for bst3 knockout.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics referenced from prior study (Fang et al. 2012); interactome referenced from prior study (Mackinder et al. 2017); accession numbers NA

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: cia5 mutant; D66 wild type background used for comparison
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports CO2 concentration around Rubisco and efficient photosynthetic carbon fixation under low CO2
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BST1; BST2; BST3; multiple CCM genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or is required for induction of BST1-3 under low/ambient CO2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: CO2 concentrating mechanism gene induction and inorganic carbon uptake/acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent master regulator required for induction of CCM components under low CO2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed genetically through cia5 mutant lacking CCM induction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semiquantitative RT-PCR comparing D66 and cia5 under high vs ambient CO2; prior literature on CIA5/CCM1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CIA5 abolishes or reduces induction of BST1-3 in ambient/low CO2, linking BST expression to CCM regulatory control
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High CO2 (5%); ambient CO2 (0.04%); low CO2 induction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream regulatory module for coordinated activation of multiple CCM genes during engineering of algal CCM traits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CIA5 is required for normal low-CO2 induction of BST1-3. 2. In cia5 mutants, BST1 and BST3 expression is severely reduced and BST2 induction is diminished. 3. This places BST1-3 within the canonical CCM transcriptional program.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CIA5 was not the primary experimental focus of the Results; included because this paper directly tests its effect on BST1-3 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Prior transcriptomic study cited (Fang et al. 2012); accession numbers NA
